hypotheses.text: End-to-end deep learning models trained on raw eye-tracking gaze coordinates outperform hand-engineered feature-based classifiers for ADHD screening.
hypotheses.rationale: Deep learning approaches have shown promise in time-series classification but have rarely been applied to raw eye-tracking data for ADHD diagnostics.

hypotheses.text: A convolutional neural network operating on spatiotemporal gaze heatmaps can achieve >85% accuracy in ADHD versus control classification without requiring manual feature engineering.
hypotheses.rationale: CNN-based approaches for gaze heatmap analysis are emerging in neuroscience but have not been benchmarked against traditional feature-based methods in ADHD populations.
